title: Most Americans Say “Not in My Backyard” to AI Data Centers
excerpt: But a Redfin analysis found that AI data centers have benefits: In northern Virginia, taxes on data center equipment are likely contributing to a surge in education spending.
content_html:
• 53% of U.S. residents oppose building a data center in their area, while 34% support it, per a recent Redfin survey. 
  • U.S. residents are more likely to oppose data centers in their neighborhood than any other type of building; for instance, 39% oppose a new apartment complex nearby. 
  • AI data centers are controversial because they strain electricity and water resources, and can disrupt communities with noise and large structures. 
  • But a Redfin analysis found that AI data centers have benefits: In northern Virginia, taxes on data center equipment are likely contributing to a surge in education spending. Meanwhile, individual homeowners’ property-tax rates are declining. 
  • Data center hubs Loudoun County, VA and Prince William County, VA have seen education spending nearly double over the last decade.

More than half (53%) of U.S. residents oppose the construction of an AI data center in their neighborhood, according to a recent Redfin survey. Roughly one-third (34%) support it. 

This is according to a Redfin-commissioned survey conducted by Ipsos in May 2026, fielded to 4,000 U.S. residents. The results for this combined group of survey respondents have a credibility interval of +/-1.9 percentage points. This report focuses on a question about how respondents feel about building certain types of properties in their neighborhoods. The building types are listed in the chart in the final section of this report. Respondents could select “strongly support,” “somewhat support,” “strongly oppose,” “somewhat oppose,” or “no opinion.” For this report, we combined the strongly/somewhat responses. 

AI data centers are controversial largely because they strain electricity and water resources, which can push up energy costs and spark environmental concerns, per various news sources. They can also disrupt communities with noise and large, industrial-looking structures. One example of opposition is New York’s recent statewide ban on building large data centers. For some people, AI data centers also represent broader fears about AI: Nearly three in five (58%) of U.S. residents believe that advances in AI will eliminate jobs and make it harder to afford homes, according to the same survey. 

“A buyer recently asked me, unprompted, to make sure there wasn’t a data center planned near a home she was considering,” said Matt Ferris, a Redfin Premier agent in northern Virginia. “I have another client who recently moved into a community in Gainesville, in Prince William County, where there are several data centers. About a year after they moved in, they said they probably would have chosen a different location had they known there were so many data centers nearby. People are concerned about noise, increased traffic, heavy water and electricity use, and how a large industrial building might change the feel of their neighborhood. Right or wrong, the perception is that data centers will change neighborhoods in a negative way.”

Broken down by age, older generations are more likely than younger generations to oppose data centers in their area. Roughly two-thirds (65%) of baby boomers and 60% of Gen Xers oppose the construction of an AI data center in their neighborhood, compared with 42% of Gen Zers and 43% of millennials. 

Additionally, Americans are more likely to oppose data centers in their neighborhood than any other type of building we asked about. More than half of U.S. residents oppose construction of a data center near their home, while 39% oppose a new apartment complex and 32% oppose a mixed-use development. Forty-eight percent oppose converting single-family homes into smaller dwellings to increase supply in their area. 

AI Data Centers Are Helping Fund Virginia Schools 

While the majority of Americans don’t want to live near AI data centers, the structures can provide educational benefits to communities. 

The boom in AI data centers in northern Virginia is helping fund public schools through a surge in tax revenue. A Redfin analysis of county financial records found that Virginia’s two largest data-center hubs—Loudoun County and Prince William County—have seen far faster growth in education spending than neighboring counties over the last 15 years. Both counties collect substantial taxes on computer equipment housed inside data centers; Loudoun’s financial reports identify computer equipment in data centers as the largest source of personal property tax revenue, which is paid by the companies responsible for them. 

Loudoun County is home to 176 data centers, more than twice the number of any other U.S. county. Neighboring Prince William County ranks third nationally, with 77 facilities. Santa Clara County, CA is second, with 83 data centers; we didn’t include Santa Clara County in this analysis of education spending because data centers aren’t a major source of revenue for that area. 

This analysis found that AI data centers are driving a tax windfall in Loudoun and Prince William counties. Personal property tax revenue, which are levies imposed on physical assets—like AI data centers—per resident has increased nearly 639% in Loudoun County over the last 15 years, and it rose 349% in Prince William County. That’s compared with 91% growth in neighboring Fairfax County, which has the fifth-most data centers in the U.S. but far fewer than Prince William County or Loudon County.

Increased tax revenue from data centers has contributed to a surge in education spending in Prince William and Loudoun counties. Prince William County increased education spending per resident by 82% to $1,589 over the last 15 years. Loudoun County increased it by 77% to $2,955 per resident. For comparison, Fairfax County increased education spending per resident by 49% to $2,234 over that period. Stafford County, VA, where there is just one operational data center, increased it by 29% to $1,024.  

Impact of AI Data Centers on Education Spending

Select Northern Virginia counties
Loudoun County, VA Prince William County, VA Fairfax County, VA Stafford County, VA
Number of data centers 176 

(most in the U.S.)

 77 

(third-most in the U.S.)

 45 

(fifth-most in the U.S.)

 1
Increase in personal property tax revenue, 2010-2025 639% 349% 91%
Increase in education spending per resident, 2010-2025 77% 82% 49% 29%
Education spending per resident, 2025 $2,955 $1,589 $2,234 $1,024

Local officials from Loudoun County have linked revenue from AI data centers to school funding. The county website says, “Over the past decade, revenue growth from data centers has allowed the county to address increasing service needs for Loudoun County Public Schools and to fund improved services to our residents while consistently lowering the real property tax rate.”

Average teacher salaries have risen more rapidly in Loudoun County than in neighboring Fairfax County, which has far fewer data centers. Teacher salaries in Loudoun County rose roughly 40% to about $83,000 from 2010 to 2023; that’s compared with a 29% increase to about $82,000 in Fairfax County. Still, teacher pay in Virginia is lagging behind the U.S. as a whole. 

Virginia recently approved a new statewide tax on data center power consumption, which would go to the state’s general fund. 

Virginia’s Data Center-Heavy Counties Are Collecting More Tax Revenue While Cutting Homeowner Rates

Loudoun and Prince William counties are seeing more revenue growth from personal property taxes than counties with fewer or no data centers. 

Meanwhile, the property-tax rate for individual homeowners has steadily fallen over the past decade. 

“Loudoun County’s expanding data-center tax base has given local officials more capacity to invest in schools, including higher education spending and teacher compensation, without upping tax rates on homeowners,” said Redfin Economist Yingqi Xu. “Because data centers are taxed largely through personal property taxes on computer equipment, that revenue can help fund growing budgets without putting the same pressure on residential real estate taxes. Surging data-center revenue allows counties to rely less heavily on homeowners to fund education, though it’s important to note that a surge in data-center revenue doesn’t automatically translate dollar-for-dollar to public spending.”

Prince William County cut its real-property tax rate from $1.12 per $100 of assessed value in 2022 to $0.92 in 2025. Due to that decline, the county has kept its estimated residential tax levy relatively flat at roughly $1,320 per resident since 2023.

Loudoun County cut its real-property tax rate from 0.89%  in 2022 to 0.81%  in 2025. But rising home values have offset those rate cuts: Redfin estimates that the county’s residential property-tax levy per resident increased from roughly $1,574 in 2014 to about $2,005 in 2024 before leveling off in 2025.

Matt Ferris, the Redfin agent in northern Virginia, says homebuyers and sellers aren’t concerned about tax revenue going toward education spending. “Prince William, Loudoun, Fairfax, and other counties in this area already have pretty well-funded schools. Residents are more worried about quality of life than a little extra money in county coffers,” he said.

Here’s a video featuring Matt Ferris and Redfin Chief Economist Daryl Fairweather:

Methodology

Much of this report is based on a Redfin analysis of county Annual Comprehensive Financial Reports (ACFRs) for select Virginia and Maryland counties—covering fiscal years 2010 through 2025. From each county’s ACFR we collected real and personal property tax revenue, education spending, residential assessed value, and real property and computer-equipment (data center) tax rates, and combined these with U.S. Census Bureau population estimates to calculate per-resident figures and growth rates. Per-capita measures divide each metric by county population; the estimated residential tax levy is calculated as residential assessed value multiplied by the real property tax rate. Data-center counts by county are from a Business Insider compilation of U.S. data-center locations (2025). 

The survey results in this report are from a survey commissioned by Rocket Mortgage and Redfin, and conducted by Ipsos, in May 2026. It was fielded to a nationally representative sample of 4,000 adults 18+ who are U.S. residents. The results for this combined group of survey respondents have a credibility interval of +/-1.9 percentage points. This report focuses on a question about how respondents feel about building certain types of properties in their neighborhoods. The building types are listed in the chart in the final section of this report. Respondents could select “strongly support,” “somewhat support,” “strongly oppose,” “somewhat oppose,” or “no opinion.” For this report, we combined the strongly/somewhat responses.

sentences.zh: 此外，与我们询问的任何其他类型的建筑相比，美国人更有可能反对在他们的社区建立数据中心。

sentences.en: More than half of U.S. residents oppose construction of a data center near their home, while 39% oppose a new apartment complex and 32% oppose a mixed-use development.
sentences.zh: 超过一半的美国居民反对在他们家附近建设数据中心，39% 的人反对建造新的公寓大楼，32% 的人反对混合用途开发。

sentences.en: Forty-eight percent oppose converting single-family homes into smaller dwellings to increase supply in their area.
sentences.zh: 48% 的人反对将单户住宅改造成较小的住宅以增加其所在地区的供应。

blocks.html_en: AI Data Centers Are Helping Fund Virginia Schools

sentences.en: AI Data Centers Are Helping Fund Virginia Schools
sentences.zh: 人工智能数据中心正在帮助资助弗吉尼亚学校

blocks.html_en: While the majority of Americans don’t want to live near AI data centers, the structures can provide educational benefits to communities.

sentences.en: While the majority of Americans don’t want to live near AI data centers, the structures can provide educational benefits to communities.
sentences.zh: 虽然大多数美国人不想住在人工智能数据中心附近，但这些结构可以为社区提供教育效益。

blocks.html_en: The boom in AI data centers in northern Virginia is helping fund public schools through a surge in tax revenue. A Redfin analysis of county financial records found that Virginia’s two largest data-center hubs—Loudoun County and Prince William County—have seen far faster growth in education spending than neighboring counties over the last 15 years. Both counties collect substantial taxes on computer equipment housed inside data centers; Loudoun’s financial reports identify computer equipment in data centers as the largest source of personal property tax revenue, which is paid by the companies responsible for them.

sentences.en: The boom in AI data centers in northern Virginia is helping fund public schools through a surge in tax revenue.
sentences.zh: 弗吉尼亚州北部人工智能数据中心的蓬勃发展正在通过税收收入的激增为公立学校提供资金。

sentences.en: A Redfin analysis of county financial records found that Virginia’s two largest data-center hubs—Loudoun County and Prince William County—have seen far faster growth in education spending than neighboring counties over the last 15 years.
sentences.zh: Redfin 对县财务记录的分析发现，弗吉尼亚州最大的两个数据中心——劳登县和威廉王子县——在过去 15 年中教育支出的增长速度远远快于邻近县。

sentences.en: Both counties collect substantial taxes on computer equipment housed inside data centers; Loudoun’s financial reports identify computer equipment in data centers as the largest source of personal property tax revenue, which is paid by the companies responsible for them.
sentences.zh: 这两个县都对数据中心内的计算机设备征收高额税款；劳登的财务报告将数据中心的计算机设备视为个人财产税收入的最大来源，由负责这些设备的公司支付。

blocks.html_en: Loudoun County is home to 176 data centers, more than twice the number of any other U.S. county. Neighboring Prince William County ranks third nationally, with 77 facilities. Santa Clara County, CA is second, with 83 data centers; we didn’t include Santa Clara County in this analysis of education spending because data centers aren’t a major source of revenue for that area.

sentences.en: Loudoun County is home to 176 data centers, more than twice the number of any other U.S. county.
sentences.zh: 劳登县拥有 176 个数据中心，是美国其他县的两倍多。

sentences.en: Neighboring Prince William County ranks third nationally, with 77 facilities.
sentences.zh: 邻近的威廉王子县拥有 77 个设施，位居全国第三。

sentences.en: Santa Clara County, CA is second, with 83 data centers; we didn’t include Santa Clara County in this analysis of education spending because data centers aren’t a major source of revenue for that area.
sentences.zh: 加利福尼亚州圣克拉拉县排名第二，拥有 83 个数据中心；我们没有将圣克拉拉县纳入教育支出分析，因为数据中心并不是该地区的主要收入来源。

blocks.html_en: This analysis found that AI data centers are driving a tax windfall in Loudoun and Prince William counties. Personal property tax revenue, which are levies imposed on physical assets—like AI data centers—per resident has increased nearly 639% in Loudoun County over the last 15 years, and it rose 349% in Prince William County. That’s compared with 91% growth in neighboring Fairfax County, which has the fifth-most data centers in the U.S. but far fewer than Prince William County or Loudon County.

sentences.en: This analysis found that AI data centers are driving a tax windfall in Loudoun and Prince William counties.
sentences.zh: 这项分析发现，人工智能数据中心正在为劳登县和威廉王子县带来巨额税收。

sentences.en: Personal property tax revenue, which are levies imposed on physical assets—like AI data centers—per resident has increased nearly 639% in Loudoun County over the last 15 years, and it rose 349% in Prince William County.
sentences.zh: 过去 15 年中，劳登县的个人财产税收入（对 AI 数据中心等实物资产征收的税）增加了近 639%，威廉王子县的个人财产税增加了 349%。

sentences.en: That’s compared with 91% growth in neighboring Fairfax County, which has the fifth-most data centers in the U.S. but far fewer than Prince William County or Loudon County.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，邻近的费尔法克斯县增长了 91%，该县的数据中心数量在美国排名第五，但远低于威廉王子县或劳登县。

blocks.html_en: Increased tax revenue from data centers has contributed to a surge in education spending in Prince William and Loudoun counties. Prince William County increased education spending per resident by 82% to $1,589 over the last 15 years. Loudoun County increased it by 77% to $2,955 per resident. For comparison, Fairfax County increased education spending per resident by 49% to $2,234 over that period. Stafford County, VA, where there is just one operational data center, increased it by 29% to $1,024.

sentences.en: Increased tax revenue from data centers has contributed to a surge in education spending in Prince William and Loudoun counties.
sentences.zh: 数据中心税收的增加推动了威廉王子县和劳登县的教育支出激增。

sentences.en: Prince William County increased education spending per resident by 82% to $1,589 over the last 15 years.
sentences.zh: 过去 15 年里，威廉王子县居民的人均教育支出增加了 82%，达到 1,589 美元。

sentences.en: Loudoun County increased it by 77% to $2,955 per resident.
sentences.zh: 劳登县增加了 77%，达到每位居民 2,955 美元。

sentences.en: For comparison, Fairfax County increased education spending per resident by 49% to $2,234 over that period.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，费尔法克斯县同期居民人均教育支出增加了 49%，达到 2,234 美元。

sentences.en: Stafford County, VA, where there is just one operational data center, increased it by 29% to $1,024.
sentences.zh: 弗吉尼亚州斯塔福德县只有一个运营数据中心，价格上涨了 29% 至 1,024 美元。

Local officials from Loudoun County have linked revenue from AI data centers to school funding. The county website says, “Over the past decade, revenue growth from data centers has allowed the county to address increasing service needs for Loudoun County Public Schools and to fund improved services to our residents while consistently lowering the real property tax rate.”

sentences.en: Local officials from Loudoun County have linked revenue from AI data centers to school funding.
sentences.zh: 劳登县当地官员已将人工智能数据中心的收入与学校经费挂钩。

sentences.en: The county website says, “Over the past decade, revenue growth from data centers has allowed the county to address increasing service needs for Loudoun County Public Schools and to fund improved services to our residents while consistently lowering the real property tax rate.”
sentences.zh: 该县网站称：“过去十年，数据中心的收入增长使该县能够满足劳登县公立学校日益增长的服务需求，并为改善居民服务提供资金，同时不断降低房地产税率。”

blocks.html_en: Average teacher salaries have risen more rapidly in Loudoun County than in neighboring Fairfax County, which has far fewer data centers. Teacher salaries in Loudoun County rose roughly 40% to about $83,000 from 2010 to 2023; that’s compared with a 29% increase to about $82,000 in Fairfax County. Still, teacher pay in Virginia is lagging behind the U.S. as a whole.

sentences.en: Average teacher salaries have risen more rapidly in Loudoun County than in neighboring Fairfax County, which has far fewer data centers.
sentences.zh: 劳登县教师平均工资的增长速度比邻近的费尔法克斯县要快，后者的数据中心要少得多。

sentences.en: Teacher salaries in Loudoun County rose roughly 40% to about $83,000 from 2010 to 2023; that’s compared with a 29% increase to about $82,000 in Fairfax County.
sentences.zh: 从 2010 年到 2023 年，劳登县教师的工资上涨了约 40%，达到约 83,000 美元；相比之下，费尔法克斯县的房价上涨了 29%，达到约 82,000 美元。

sentences.en: Still, teacher pay in Virginia is lagging behind the U.S. as a whole.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，弗吉尼亚州的教师工资仍落后于美国整体。

blocks.html_en: Virginia recently approved a new statewide tax on data center power consumption, which would go to the state’s general fund.

sentences.en: Virginia recently approved a new statewide tax on data center power consumption, which would go to the state’s general fund.
sentences.zh: 弗吉尼亚州最近批准了一项新的全州范围内的数据中心电力消耗税，该税将流入该州的普通基金。

blocks.html_en: Virginia’s Data Center-Heavy Counties Are Collecting More Tax Revenue While Cutting Homeowner Rates

sentences.en: Virginia’s Data Center-Heavy Counties Are Collecting More Tax Revenue While Cutting Homeowner Rates
sentences.zh: 弗吉尼亚州数据中心密集的县正在征收更多税收，同时降低房主税率

blocks.html_en: Loudoun and Prince William counties are seeing more revenue growth from personal property taxes than counties with fewer or no data centers.

sentences.en: Loudoun and Prince William counties are seeing more revenue growth from personal property taxes than counties with fewer or no data centers.
sentences.zh: 与数据中心较少或没有数据中心的县相比，劳登县和威廉王子县的个人财产税收入增长更多。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, the property-tax rate for individual homeowners has steadily fallen over the past decade.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, the property-tax rate for individual homeowners has steadily fallen over the past decade.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，个人房主的财产税率在过去十年中稳步下降。

blocks.html_en: “Loudoun County’s expanding data-center tax base has given local officials more capacity to invest in schools, including higher education spending and teacher compensation, without upping tax rates on homeowners,” said Redfin Economist Yingqi Xu. “Because data centers are taxed largely through personal property taxes on computer equipment, that revenue can help fund growing budgets without putting the same pressure on residential real estate taxes. Surging data-center revenue allows counties to rely less heavily on homeowners to fund education, though it’s important to note that a surge in data-center revenue doesn’t automatically translate dollar-for-dollar to public spending.”

sentences.en: “Loudoun County’s expanding data-center tax base has given local officials more capacity to invest in schools, including higher education spending and teacher compensation, without upping tax rates on homeowners,” said Redfin Economist Yingqi Xu.
sentences.zh: Redfin 经济学家 Yingqi Xu 表示：“劳登县不断扩大的数据中心税基使地方官员有更多能力投资于学校，包括高等教育支出和教师薪酬，而无需提高房主的税率。”

sentences.en: “Because data centers are taxed largely through personal property taxes on computer equipment, that revenue can help fund growing budgets without putting the same pressure on residential real estate taxes.
sentences.zh: “由于数据中心主要通过计算机设备的个人财产税来征税，因此这些收入可以帮助为不断增长的预算提供资金，而不会给住宅房地产税带来同样的压力。

sentences.en: Surging data-center revenue allows counties to rely less heavily on homeowners to fund education, though it’s important to note that a surge in data-center revenue doesn’t automatically translate dollar-for-dollar to public spending.”
sentences.zh: 数据中心收入的激增使各县可以减少对房主资助教育的依赖，但值得注意的是，数据中心收入的激增并不会自动转化为公共支出。”

blocks.html_en: Prince William County cut its real-property tax rate from $1.12 per $100 of assessed value in 2022 to $0.92 in 2025. Due to that decline, the county has kept its estimated residential tax levy relatively flat at roughly $1,320 per resident since 2023.

sentences.en: Prince William County cut its real-property tax rate from $1.12 per $100 of assessed value in 2022 to $0.92 in 2025.
sentences.zh: 威廉王子县将其不动产税率从 2022 年的每 100 美元评估价值 1.12 美元下调至 2025 年的 0.92 美元。

sentences.en: Due to that decline, the county has kept its estimated residential tax levy relatively flat at roughly $1,320 per resident since 2023.
sentences.zh: 由于这一下降，该县自 2023 年以来一直将预计的住宅税保持在每位居民约 1,320 美元的水平上。

blocks.html_en: Loudoun County cut its real-property tax rate from 0.89%  in 2022 to 0.81%  in 2025. But rising home values have offset those rate cuts: Redfin estimates that the county’s residential property-tax levy per resident increased from roughly $1,574 in 2014 to about $2,005 in 2024 before leveling off in 2025.

sentences.en: Loudoun County cut its real-property tax rate from 0.89% in 2022 to 0.81% in 2025.
sentences.zh: 劳登县将不动产税率从 2022 年的 0.89% 降至 2025 年的 0.81%。

sentences.en: But rising home values have offset those rate cuts: Redfin estimates that the county’s residential property-tax levy per resident increased from roughly $1,574 in 2014 to about $2,005 in 2024 before leveling off in 2025.
sentences.zh: 但房价上涨抵消了这些降税：Redfin 估计，该县每名居民的住宅房产税从 2014 年的约 1,574 美元增至 2024 年的约 2,005 美元，然后在 2025 年趋于平稳。

blocks.html_en: Matt Ferris, the Redfin agent in northern Virginia, says homebuyers and sellers aren’t concerned about tax revenue going toward education spending. “Prince William, Loudoun, Fairfax, and other counties in this area already have pretty well-funded schools. Residents are more worried about quality of life than a little extra money in county coffers,” he said.

sentences.en: Matt Ferris, the Redfin agent in northern Virginia, says homebuyers and sellers aren’t concerned about tax revenue going toward education spending.
sentences.zh: 弗吉尼亚州北部 Redfin 经纪人马特·费里斯 (Matt Ferris) 表示，购房者和卖家并不担心税收收入用于教育支出。

sentences.en: “Prince William, Loudoun, Fairfax, and other counties in this area already have pretty well-funded schools.
sentences.zh: “威廉王子城、劳登县、费尔法克斯县和该地区的其他县已经拥有资金相当充足的学校。

sentences.en: Residents are more worried about quality of life than a little extra money in county coffers,” he said.
sentences.zh: 居民们更担心的是生活质量，而不是县库里的一点额外钱，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Methodology

sentences.en: Methodology
sentences.zh: 方法论

blocks.html_en: Much of this report is based on a Redfin analysis of county Annual Comprehensive Financial Reports (ACFRs) for select Virginia and Maryland counties—covering fiscal years 2010 through 2025. From each county’s ACFR we collected real and personal property tax revenue, education spending, residential assessed value, and real property and computer-equipment (data center) tax rates, and combined these with U.S. Census Bureau population estimates to calculate per-resident figures and growth rates. Per-capita measures divide each metric by county population; the estimated residential tax levy is calculated as residential assessed value multiplied by the real property tax rate. Data-center counts by county are from a Business Insider compilation of U.S. data-center locations (2025).

sentences.en: Much of this report is based on a Redfin analysis of county Annual Comprehensive Financial Reports (ACFRs) for select Virginia and Maryland counties—covering fiscal years 2010 through 2025.
sentences.zh: 本报告的大部分内容基于 Redfin 对弗吉尼亚州和马里兰州选定县的县年度综合财务报告 (ACFR) 的分析，涵盖 2010 年至 2025 财年。

sentences.en: From each county’s ACFR we collected real and personal property tax revenue, education spending, residential assessed value, and real property and computer-equipment (data center) tax rates, and combined these with U.S. Census Bureau population estimates to calculate per-resident figures and growth rates.
sentences.zh: 我们从每个县的 ACFR 中收集了不动产和动产税收收入、教育支出、住宅评估价值以及不动产和计算机设备（数据中心）税率，并将这些数据与美国人口普查局的人口估计值相结合，计算出每名居民的数据和增长率。

sentences.en: Per-capita measures divide each metric by county population; the estimated residential tax levy is calculated as residential assessed value multiplied by the real property tax rate.
sentences.zh: 人均指标将每个指标除以县人口；预计住宅税的计算方法是住宅评估值乘以不动产税率。

sentences.en: Data-center counts by county are from a Business Insider compilation of U.S. data-center locations (2025).
sentences.zh: 按县划分的数据中心数量来自 Business Insider 对美国数据中心位置的汇编（2025 年）。

blocks.html_en: The survey results in this report are from a survey commissioned by Rocket Mortgage and Redfin, and conducted by Ipsos, in May 2026. It was fielded to a nationally representative sample of 4,000 adults 18+ who are U.S. residents. The results for this combined group of survey respondents have a credibility interval of +/-1.9 percentage points. This report focuses on a question about how respondents feel about building certain types of properties in their neighborhoods. The building types are listed in the chart in the final section of this report. Respondents could select “strongly support,” “somewhat support,” “strongly oppose,” “somewhat oppose,” or “no opinion.” For this report, we combined the strongly/somewhat responses.

sentences.en: The survey results in this report are from a survey commissioned by Rocket Mortgage and Redfin, and conducted by Ipsos, in May 2026.
sentences.zh: 本报告中的调查结果来自 Rocket Mortgage 和 Redfin 委托、益普索 (Ipsos) 于 2026 年 5 月进行的一项调查。

sentences.en: It was fielded to a nationally representative sample of 4,000 adults 18+ who are U.S. residents.
sentences.zh: 该研究针对具有全国代表性的 4,000 名 18 岁以上美国居民样本进行了调查。

sentences.en: The results for this combined group of survey respondents have a credibility interval of +/-1.9 percentage points.
sentences.zh: 这组调查受访者的综合结果的可信区间为 +/-1.9 个百分点。

sentences.en: This report focuses on a question about how respondents feel about building certain types of properties in their neighborhoods.
sentences.zh: 本报告重点关注受访者对在其社区建造某些类型的房产有何看法的问题。

sentences.en: The building types are listed in the chart in the final section of this report.
sentences.zh: 本报告最后部分的图表列出了建筑类型。

sentences.en: Respondents could select “strongly support,” “somewhat support,” “strongly oppose,” “somewhat oppose,” or “no opinion.”
sentences.zh: 受访者可以选择“强烈支持”、“有些支持”、“强烈反对”、“有些反对”或“无意见”。

sentences.en: For this report, we combined the strongly/somewhat responses.
sentences.zh: 在这份报告中，我们综合了强烈/有些反应。

content_html_zh:
• 根据 Redfin 最近的一项调查，53% 的美国居民反对在他们所在地区建立数据中心，而 34% 的人支持。
  • 与任何其他类型的建筑相比，美国居民更有可能反对在他们的社区建立数据中心；例如，39%的人反对在附近建造新的公寓大楼。
  • 人工智能数据中心是有争议的，因为它们会给电力和水资源带来压力，并且会因噪音和大型建筑而扰乱社区。
  • 但 Redfin 的一项分析发现，人工智能数据中心有好处：在弗吉尼亚州北部，对数据中心设备征税可能会导致教育支出激增。 与此同时，个人房主的财产税率正在下降。
  • 过去十年，弗吉尼亚州劳登县和威廉王子县的数据中心中心的教育支出几乎翻了一番。

Redfin 最近的一项调查显示，超过一半 (53%) 的美国居民反对在他们的社区建设人工智能数据中心。 大约三分之一（34%）的人支持。

这是根据 Redfin 委托 Ipsos 于 2026 年 5 月对 4,000 名美国居民进行的调查得出的。 这组调查受访者的综合结果的可信区间为 +/-1.9 个百分点。 本报告重点关注受访者对在其社区建造某些类型的房产有何看法的问题。 本报告最后部分的图表列出了建筑类型。 受访者可以选择“强烈支持”、“有些支持”、“强烈反对”、“有些反对”或“无意见”。 在这份报告中，我们综合了强烈/有些反应。

根据各种新闻来源，人工智能数据中心之所以备受争议，很大程度上是因为它们对电力和水资源造成压力，从而推高能源成本并引发环境问题。 它们还可能通过噪音和大型工业建筑扰乱社区。 反对的例子之一是纽约最近在全州范围内禁止建设大型数据中心。 对于一些人来说，人工智能数据中心也代表了对人工智能更广泛的恐惧：根据同一项调查，近五分之三（58%）的美国居民认为人工智能的进步将消除就业机会，并使买房变得更加困难。

“一位买家最近主动要求我确保她正在考虑的房屋附近没有计划建设数据中心，”弗吉尼亚州北部 Redfin Premier 经纪人马特·费里斯 (Matt Ferris) 说。 “我的另一位客户最近搬进了威廉王子县盖恩斯维尔的一个社区，那里有几个数据中心。 搬入大约一年后，他们表示，如果知道附近有这么多数据中心，他们可能会选择不同的地点。 人们担心噪音、交通流量增加、水电消耗过多，以及大型工业建筑可能会如何改变他们的社区感觉。 无论正确与否，人们的看法是数据中心将以负面的方式改变社区。”

按年龄划分，老一辈比年轻一代更有可能反对在其所在地区建立数据中心。 大约三分之二 (65%) 的婴儿潮一代和 60% 的 X 一代反对在他们的社区建设人工智能数据中心，而 Z 一代和千禧一代的这一比例分别为 42% 和 43%。

此外，与我们询问的任何其他类型的建筑相比，美国人更有可能反对在他们的社区建立数据中心。 超过一半的美国居民反对在他们家附近建设数据中心，39% 的人反对建造新的公寓大楼，32% 的人反对混合用途开发。 48% 的人反对将单户住宅改造成较小的住宅以增加其所在地区的供应。

人工智能数据中心正在帮助资助弗吉尼亚学校

虽然大多数美国人不想住在人工智能数据中心附近，但这些结构可以为社区提供教育效益。

弗吉尼亚州北部人工智能数据中心的蓬勃发展正在通过税收收入的激增为公立学校提供资金。 Redfin 对县财务记录的分析发现，弗吉尼亚州最大的两个数据中心——劳登县和威廉王子县——在过去 15 年中教育支出的增长速度远远快于邻近县。 这两个县都对数据中心内的计算机设备征收高额税款；劳登的财务报告将数据中心的计算机设备视为个人财产税收入的最大来源，由负责这些设备的公司支付。

劳登县拥有 176 个数据中心，是美国其他县的两倍多。 邻近的威廉王子县拥有 77 个设施，位居全国第三。 加利福尼亚州圣克拉拉县排名第二，拥有 83 个数据中心；我们没有将圣克拉拉县纳入教育支出分析，因为数据中心并不是该地区的主要收入来源。

这项分析发现，人工智能数据中心正在为劳登县和威廉王子县带来巨额税收。 过去 15 年中，劳登县的个人财产税收入（对 AI 数据中心等实物资产征收的税）增加了近 639%，威廉王子县的个人财产税增加了 349%。 相比之下，邻近的费尔法克斯县增长了 91%，该县的数据中心数量在美国排名第五，但远低于威廉王子县或劳登县。

数据中心税收的增加推动了威廉王子县和劳登县的教育支出激增。 过去 15 年里，威廉王子县居民的人均教育支出增加了 82%，达到 1,589 美元。 劳登县增加了 77%，达到每位居民 2,955 美元。 相比之下，费尔法克斯县同期居民人均教育支出增加了 49%，达到 2,234 美元。 弗吉尼亚州斯塔福德县只有一个运营数据中心，价格上涨了 29% 至 1,024 美元。

人工智能数据中心对弗吉尼亚北部部分县教育支出的影响
弗吉尼亚州劳登县 弗吉尼亚州威廉王子县 弗吉尼亚州费尔法克斯县 弗吉尼亚州斯塔福德县
数据中心数量 176（大多数在美国） 77（美国第三多） 45（美国第五） 1
2010-2025 年个人财产税收入增长 639% 349% 91%
2010-2025 年居民人均教育支出增加 77% 82% 49% 29%
2025 年居民人均教育支出 $2,955 $1,589 $2,234 $1,024

劳登县当地官员已将人工智能数据中心的收入与学校经费挂钩。 该县网站称：“过去十年，数据中心的收入增长使该县能够满足劳登县公立学校日益增长的服务需求，并为改善居民服务提供资金，同时不断降低房地产税率。”

劳登县教师平均工资的增长速度比邻近的费尔法克斯县要快，后者的数据中心要少得多。 从 2010 年到 2023 年，劳登县教师的工资上涨了约 40%，达到约 83,000 美元；相比之下，费尔法克斯县的房价上涨了 29%，达到约 82,000 美元。 尽管如此，弗吉尼亚州的教师工资仍落后于美国整体。

弗吉尼亚州最近批准了一项新的全州范围内的数据中心电力消耗税，该税将流入该州的普通基金。

弗吉尼亚州数据中心密集的县正在征收更多税收，同时降低房主税率

与数据中心较少或没有数据中心的县相比，劳登县和威廉王子县的个人财产税收入增长更多。

与此同时，个人房主的财产税率在过去十年中稳步下降。

Redfin 经济学家 Yingqi Xu 表示：“劳登县不断扩大的数据中心税基使地方官员有更多能力投资于学校，包括高等教育支出和教师薪酬，而无需提高房主的税率。” “由于数据中心主要通过计算机设备的个人财产税来征税，因此这些收入可以帮助为不断增长的预算提供资金，而不会给住宅房地产税带来同样的压力。 数据中心收入的激增使各县可以减少对房主资助教育的依赖，但值得注意的是，数据中心收入的激增并不会自动转化为公共支出。”

威廉王子县将其不动产税率从 2022 年的每 100 美元评估价值 1.12 美元下调至 2025 年的 0.92 美元。 由于这一下降，该县自 2023 年以来一直将预计的住宅税保持在每位居民约 1,320 美元的水平上。

劳登县将不动产税率从 2022 年的 0.89% 降至 2025 年的 0.81%。 但房价上涨抵消了这些降税：Redfin 估计，该县每名居民的住宅房产税从 2014 年的约 1,574 美元增至 2024 年的约 2,005 美元，然后在 2025 年趋于平稳。

弗吉尼亚州北部 Redfin 经纪人马特·费里斯 (Matt Ferris) 表示，购房者和卖家并不担心税收收入用于教育支出。 “威廉王子城、劳登县、费尔法克斯县和该地区的其他县已经拥有资金相当充足的学校。 居民们更担心的是生活质量，而不是县库里的一点额外钱，”他说。

方法论

本报告的大部分内容基于 Redfin 对弗吉尼亚州和马里兰州选定县的县年度综合财务报告 (ACFR) 的分析，涵盖 2010 年至 2025 财年。 我们从每个县的 ACFR 中收集了不动产和动产税收收入、教育支出、住宅评估价值以及不动产和计算机设备（数据中心）税率，并将这些数据与美国人口普查局的人口估计值相结合，计算出每名居民的数据和增长率。 人均指标将每个指标除以县人口；预计住宅税的计算方法是住宅评估值乘以不动产税率。 按县划分的数据中心数量来自 Business Insider 对美国数据中心位置的汇编（2025 年）。

本报告中的调查结果来自 Rocket Mortgage 和 Redfin 委托、益普索 (Ipsos) 于 2026 年 5 月进行的一项调查。 该研究针对具有全国代表性的 4,000 名 18 岁以上美国居民样本进行了调查。 这组调查受访者的综合结果的可信区间为 +/-1.9 个百分点。 本报告重点关注受访者对在其社区建造某些类型的房产有何看法的问题。 本报告最后部分的图表列出了建筑类型。 受访者可以选择“强烈支持”、“有些支持”、“强烈反对”、“有些反对”或“无意见”。 在这份报告中，我们综合了强烈/有些反应。
